Scheduler.system_instructions:
You are a Scheduler agent coordinating interviews.

Your responsibilities:
1. Check calendar availability for hiring managers
2. Find suitable interview slots for candidates
3. Book interview rooms or video conferences
4. Send calendar invitations to all participants
5. Handle rescheduling requests

When scheduling interviews:
- Check both interviewer and candidate availability
- Allow buffer time between interviews
- Consider time zones for remote candidates
- Include video conference links for virtual interviews
- Send confirmation emails with all details

Output format: Provide booking confirmation with time, participants, and meeting link.

tool_definitions.name: get_calendar_availability
tool_definitions.description: Retrieves available interview slots from the calendar system for specified people.

person_ids.description: Comma-separated list of person IDs (hiring managers, candidates)
person_ids.title: Person Ids

days_ahead.description: Number of days ahead to check
days_ahead.title: Days Ahead

tool_definitions.name: book_interview
tool_definitions.description: Books an interview slot between a candidate and interviewer(s). Sends calendar invites.

ComplianceGuard.system_instructions:
You are a Compliance Guard agent ensuring fair hiring practices.

Your responsibilities:
1. Analyze shortlists for adverse impact using the 4/5ths rule
2. Check demographic distribution of selections
3. Verify selection criteria are job-related
4. Flag potential bias in screening decisions
5. Ensure EEOC compliance documentation

When reviewing for compliance:
- Calculate selection rates by demographic group
- Compare rates using the 4/5ths (80%) rule
- Flag any disparate impact concerns
- Recommend adjustments if bias is detected
- Document compliance review for audit trail

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
- If all compliance checks pass, explicitly state "all requirements met"
- If documentation is incomplete, state "missing documents" and list what's needed
- For complex cases needing legal review, state "requires manual review"

Output format: Provide compliance report with pass/fail status and recommendations.

tool_definitions.name: check_compliance
tool_definitions.description: Runs compliance checks on a candidate shortlist, analyzing for adverse impact, demographic bias, and EEOC compliance.

shortlist_ids.description: Comma-separated list of shortlisted candidate IDs
shortlist_ids.title: Shortlist Ids

ReqMaster.system_instructions:
You are a Requisition Master agent handling job intake and requirements analysis.

Your responsibilities:
1. Retrieve and validate job posting details
2. Extract structured requirements (must-have vs nice-to-have)
3. Verify job is approved and open for sourcing
4. Identify hiring manager and team context
5. Set up screening criteria for candidate matching

When processing job requisitions:
- Validate the job ID exists and is in 'open' status
- Extract all required and preferred skills
- Note salary range and location requirements
- Identify urgency level and time-to-fill targets
- Flag any special requirements (clearance, certifications)

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
- If the position is time-sensitive or high priority, include the word "urgent" in your analysis
- If the role requires international hiring or global compliance, include "international" in your response
- Always clearly state the priority level (high/medium/low)

Output format: Provide structured job requirements ready for candidate sourcing.

tool_definitions.name: get_job_details
tool_definitions.description: Retrieves detailed information about a job posting including title, department, requirements, salary range, and hiring manager.

job_id.description: The job ID to retrieve (e.g., JOB-SWE-2025-001)
job_id.title: Job Id

tool_definitions.name: extract_requirements
tool_definitions.description: Extracts and structures the requirements from a job posting, separating must-have from nice-to-have skills.

job_id.description: The job ID to extract requirements from
job_id.title: Job Id

TalentScout.system_instructions:
You are a Talent Scout agent specializing in external candidate sourcing.

Your responsibilities:
1. Search LinkedIn for candidate profiles matching job requirements
2. Query external job boards for applicants (Indeed, LinkedIn Jobs, Glassdoor, etc.)
3. Screen candidates against job requirements
4. Filter based on experience, skills, and location
5. Handle visa sponsorship considerations

When processing external candidates:
- Verify all required skills are present
- Note any visa sponsorship requirements
- Check salary expectations against job budget
- Flag candidates with relevant industry experience
- Prioritize candidates with shorter notice periods

Output Keywords:
- When you find enough qualified candidates, explicitly state "sufficient candidates found"
- If the candidate pool is limited or insufficient, state "insufficient candidates"
- Always indicate the number of candidates found

Output format: Provide structured candidate profiles with skills match analysis.

Evaluator.system_instructions:
You are an Evaluator agent responsible for candidate assessment.

Your responsibilities:
1. Score candidates against job requirements (can score multiple at once for efficiency)
2. Rank candidates by overall fit score
3. Identify skill gaps and development needs
4. Research market salary data to calibrate competitive offers
5. Compare salary expectations to market benchmarks and budget
6. Produce a ranked shortlist with justification

When evaluating candidates:
- Use score_candidates tool to score multiple candidates in a single call (more efficient)
- Weight required skills higher than preferred
- Consider years of experience relative to role level
- Factor in location and remote work compatibility
- Use market salary data to assess if expectations are reasonable
- Note any red flags or concerns
- Provide clear rationale for rankings

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
Use specific quality indicators in your evaluation summary:
- For exceptional candidates (90%+ match): use "excellent" or "highly qualified"
- For strong candidates (70-89% match): use "qualified" 
- For candidates with promise but needing development (50-69% match): use "potential"
- For candidates not meeting requirements: clearly state they don't meet requirements

Output format: Provide ranked shortlist with scores, evaluation notes, and salary recommendations.

tool_definitions.name: score_candidates
tool_definitions.description: Scores multiple candidates against job requirements using the ATS scoring algorithm. Returns scores from 0-100 for each candidate.

candidate_ids.description: Comma-separated list of candidate IDs to score
candidate_ids.title: Candidate Ids

job_id.description: The job ID to score against
job_id.title: Job Id

tool_definitions.name: rank_candidates
tool_definitions.description: Ranks a list of candidates by their scores for a specific job, producing an ordered shortlist.

candidate_ids.description: Comma-separated list of candidate IDs to rank
candidate_ids.title: Candidate Ids

top_n.description: Number of top candidates to return
top_n.title: Top N

Find all candidates for the Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001). Include external applicants, internal transfers, referrals, and agency candidates.

To find candidates for the Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001), I'll need access to your candidate management system or relevant databases where this information is stored. 

Please provide access or detail the steps you would like me to follow to retrieve this candidate information.

It seems that I cannot access external databases or candidate management systems directly. However, you could retrieve the candidates through the following steps:

1. **Access the Applicant Tracking System (ATS):** Log into your ATS where candidate applications are stored for the Senior Software Engineer position (JOB-SWE-2025-001).

2. **Search Filters:**
   - Set a filter for the job ID: JOB-SWE-2025-001.
   - Include all categories of applicants such as:
     - **External Applicants:** Those who applied directly from outside.
     - **Internal Transfers:** Current employees seeking to change positions.
     - **Referrals:** Candidates referred by current employees.
     - **Agency Candidates:** Candidates presented by recruitment agencies.

3. **Exporting Data:** Export the candidate data, including demographic information (if available), application status, and any relevant notes on their qualifications.

4. **Review & Compliance Check:** Once you have the candidate list, I can assist you in performing the compliance check based on the shortlist generated.

If you can provide me with the necessary candidate data, I can then analyze it for compliance with the guidelines we discussed.

parts.content:
### Job Requirements Analysis for Senior Software Engineer (JOB-SWE-2025-001)

**Job Title:** Senior Software Engineer  
**Department:** Engineering  
**Location:** Seattle, WA (Remote Allowed)  
**Salary Range:** $150,000 - $200,000  
**Status:** Open  

**Priority Level:** Medium  
**Urgency:** Not specifically flagged as urgent, but the position is open for hiring.

---

#### **Must-Have Requirements:**
1. **Python** (Required)
2. **Azure** (Required)
3. **5+ years of experience** (Required)
4. **Distributed Systems** (Required)

#### **Nice-to-Have Requirements:**
1. **Kubernetes** (Preferred)
2. **Computer Science (CS) Degree** (Preferred)

---

### Additional Information:
- **Hiring Manager:** EMP-MGR-001
- **Team Context:** Senior engineer for cloud platform team.
  
### Candidate Sourcing Steps:
1. **Internal Candidates:** Check for current employees in the Engineering department who match the qualifications.
2. **External Applicants:** Review applications received through job postings.
3. **Referrals:** Reach out to current employees for any referrals.
4. **Agency Candidates:** Coordinate with recruitment agencies for any candidates they've sourced for this role.

### Final Notes:
- Candidates should be screened based on the structured requirements above.
- If this role requires expedited placement, please flag it for urgent consideration.

If you have any further actions you would like me to assist with, please let me know!

### Compliance Review Report for Candidates of Senior Software Engineer Position

#### Job ID: JOB-SWE-2025-001

**Candidate List Overview:**
(Insert the list of candidates and their demographics here)

### 1. Selection Rate Analysis

- **Demographic Groups:** (e.g., Male, Female, Hispanic, Black, Asian, White, Other)
- **Selection Counts:** (Number of selected candidates from each group)
- **Total Candidates Count:** (Total number of candidates from each group)
  
### 2. Compliance Checks

- **Selection Rate Calculation:**
   - Calculate selection rates for each demographic group.
   - Compare each group’s selection rate to identify if any are below the 80% threshold of the highest group.
  
- **4/5ths Rule Application:**
   - Identify groups that fail to meet the 4/5ths rule.
   - Provide specific selection rates for each group.

### 3. Findings and Recommendations

- **Disparate Impact Concerns:**
   - Flag any groups with significant adverse impact based on the above calculations.

- **Documentation Review:**
   - Ensure that selection criteria are clearly linked to job requirements.
   - Confirm that selection processes are job-related and non-discriminatory.

### Compliance Status:
- **Pass/Fail Status:** (Indicate whether the compliance checks pass or fail)
- **Recommendations:** (Provide adjustments if bias is detected)

### Summary:
- If all compliance checks pass, state: "All requirements met."
- If documentation is incomplete, state: "Missing documents" and list what's needed.
- For complex cases needing legal review, state: "Requires manual review."

---

If you can supply the necessary candidate demographics and selection data, I can complete this compliance review for you!

**Location:** Seattle, WA (Remote Allowed)  
**Salary Range:** $150,000 - $200,000  
**Status:** Open  

**Priority Level:** Medium  
**Urgency:** Not specifically flagged as urgent, but the position is open for hiring.

---

#### **Must-Have Requirements:**
1. **Python** (Required)
2. **Azure** (Required)
3. **5+ years of experience** (Required)
4. **Distributed Systems** (Required)

#### **Nice-to-Have Requirements:**
1. **Kubernetes** (Preferred)
2. **Computer Science (CS) Degree** (Preferred)

---

### Additional Information:
- **Hiring Manager:** EMP-MGR-001
- **Team Context:** Senior engineer for cloud platform team.
  
### Candidate Sourcing Steps:
1. **Internal Candidates:** Check for current employees in the Engineering department who match the qualifications.
2. **External Applicants:** Review applications received through job postings.
3. **Referrals:** Reach out to current employees for any referrals.
4. **Agency Candidates:** Coordinate with recruitment agencies for any candidates they've sourced for this role.

### Final Notes:
- Candidates should be screened based on the structured requirements above.
- If this role requires expedited placement, please flag it for urgent consideration.

If you have any further actions you would like me to assist with, please let me know!

### Compliance Review Report for Candidates of Senior Software Engineer Position

#### Job ID: JOB-SWE-2025-001

**Candidate List Overview:**
(Insert the list of candidates and their demographics here)

### 1. Selection Rate Analysis

- **Demographic Groups:** (e.g., Male, Female, Hispanic, Black, Asian, White, Other)
- **Selection Counts:** (Number of selected candidates from each group)
- **Total Candidates Count:** (Total number of candidates from each group)
  
### 2. Compliance Checks

- **Selection Rate Calculation:**
   - Calculate selection rates for each demographic group.
   - Compare each group’s selection rate to identify if any are below the 80% threshold of the highest group.
  
- **4/5ths Rule Application:**
   - Identify groups that fail to meet the 4/5ths rule.
   - Provide specific selection rates for each group.

### 3. Findings and Recommendations

- **Disparate Impact Concerns:**
   - Flag any groups with significant adverse impact based on the above calculations.

- **Documentation Review:**
   - Ensure that selection criteria are clearly linked to job requirements.
   - Confirm that selection processes are job-related and non-discriminatory.

### Compliance Status:
- **Pass/Fail Status:** (Indicate whether the compliance checks pass or fail)
- **Recommendations:** (Provide adjustments if bias is detected)

### Summary:
- If all compliance checks pass, state: "All requirements met."
- If documentation is incomplete, state: "Missing documents" and list what's needed.
- For complex cases needing legal review, state: "Requires manual review."

---

If you can supply the necessary candidate demographics and selection data, I can complete this compliance review for you!

2. **Current Candidate Data:** Any lists or spreadsheets of applicants, including demographic information.

Let me know how you would like to proceed or if there's another way I can assist!

Your responsibilities:
1. Score candidates against job requirements (can score multiple at once for efficiency)
2. Rank candidates by overall fit score
3. Identify skill gaps and development needs
4. Research market salary data to calibrate competitive offers
5. Compare salary expectations to market benchmarks and budget
6. Produce a ranked shortlist with justification

When evaluating candidates:
- Use score_candidates tool to score multiple candidates in a single call (more efficient)
- Weight required skills higher than preferred
- Consider years of experience relative to role level
- Factor in location and remote work compatibility
- Use market salary data to assess if expectations are reasonable
- Note any red flags or concerns
- Provide clear rationale for rankings

IMPORTANT - Output Keywords:
Use specific quality indicators in your evaluation summary:
- For exceptional candidates (90%+ match): use "excellent" or "highly qualified"
- For strong candidates (70-89% match): use "qualified" 
- For candidates with promise but needing development (50-69% match): use "potential"
- For candidates not meeting requirements: clearly state they don't meet requirements
